Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the application.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), strong password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe), date of birth (e.g., 01/01/1990), and valid phone number (e.g., +393123456789).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter strong password.
4. Confirm password.
5. Enter full name.
6. Enter date of birth.
7. Enter phone number.
8. Click "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com) and correct password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter registered email address.
3. Enter correct password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully, and the home page or dashboard is displayed.

Title: Failed User Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User has a registered account.
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com) and incorrect password (e.g., wrongpass).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter registered email address.
3. Enter incorrect password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating incorrect password. User remains on the login page.

Title: Add a New Plant to the System
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Valid plant name (e.g., Oak Tree), scientific name (e.g., Quercus robur), location (e.g., Venice, Italy), and valid date of planting (e.g., 01/01/2023).

2. Enter plant name.
3. Enter scientific name.

5. Enter date of planting.
6. Click "Add Plant" button.
Expected Result: Plant is added successfully to the user's collection, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Expected Result: System provides an analysis report indicating the health status of the plant (e.g., healthy, diseased, or needs attention).

Expected Result: System identifies potential diseases and provides a list of possible diagnoses with descriptions.

Title: Add a new plant to the personal profile
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the "Add Plant" screen
Test Data: Plant name: "Lavender", Species: "Lavandula angustifolia", Planting date: 2024-05-15, Duration: 3 years, Notes: "Needs full sun"
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Add Plant" section.

4. Enter the planting date as 2024-05-15.

6. Add notes: "Needs full sun".
7. Click the "Save" button.
Expected Result: The plant is successfully added to the user’s profile and appears in the personal diary with all entered details.

Title: Add a plant with missing required fields

Title: Verify system uptime and data accuracy
Preconditions: System is operational for 30 days
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Monitor the application for 30 consecutive days.
2. Record any downtime or interruptions.
3. Verify that all plant data (e.g., names, species, planting dates) remains accurate and unchanged.
Expected Result: The system operates without interruptions for at least 99% of the 30-day period. All plant data remains accurate and consistent.

Title: Verify data encryption and 2FA for sensitive user data
Preconditions: User account is created with sensitive data (email, password)
Test Data: User email: test@example.com, Password: SecurePass123
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to log in with incorrect credentials (e.g., wrong password).
2. Verify that the system requires 2FA (e.g., SMS code or authenticator app) for login.
3. Use a packet sniffer tool to intercept network traffic during login.
Expected Result: The system rejects incorrect credentials and enforces 2FA. All transmitted data (email, password) is encrypted in transit. No plaintext credentials are visible in intercepted traffic.

Title: Verify system tolerance to invalid input
Preconditions: User is on the "Add Plant" screen
Test Data: Planting date entered as "2024-13-45" (invalid date)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Add Plant" section.
2. Enter an invalid planting date (e.g., "2024-13-45").
3. Attempt to save the plant.
Expected Result: The system displays an error message: "Invalid date. Please enter a valid date (YYYY-MM-DD)." The plant is not saved.

Title: Verify app functionality on different Android versions
Preconditions: App is installed on devices with Android 8.0, 9.0, and 10.0
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on a device with Android 8.0.
2. Perform critical actions: Add a plant, upload a photo for disease detection, and view the personal diary.
3. Repeat steps 1-2 on devices with Android 9.0 and 10.0.
Expected Result: The app functions correctly on all tested Android versions (8.0, 9.0, 10.0) with no crashes or missing features. Performance remains consistent.

Title: Verify CPU usage during idle and intensive operations
Preconditions: Device has at least 1 GB of RAM
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and leave it idle for 5 minutes.
2. Measure CPU usage using a monitoring tool (e.g., Android Profiler).
3. Perform an intensive operation (e.g., uploading 10 plant photos for disease detection in quick succession).
4. Measure CPU usage during and after the operation.
Expected Result: CPU usage remains below 20% during idle. During intensive operations, CPU usage does not exceed 50%. The app remains responsive.

Title: Verify memory consumption during app operation
Preconditions: App installed on a target device with known total RAM capacity
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app on a target device.
2. Perform a series of operations (e.g., load data, execute queries, navigate screens).
3. Monitor RAM usage via system tools (e.g., Android Developer Options).
4. Record peak memory consumption during operations.
Expected Result: Memory usage remains below 60% of total available RAM.

Title: Measure app startup time
Preconditions: App installed on a target device
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Close the app completely.
2. Launch the app and start a timer immediately.
3. Record the time when the main screen is fully loaded and interactive.
Expected Result: Startup time is less than 3 seconds.

Title: Validate response time for standard user requests
Preconditions: App connected to Firebase with active data
Test Data: 10 standard user requests (e.g., login, data retrieval)
Test Steps:
1. Execute each of the 10 standard requests.
2. Record the response time for each request.
Expected Result: 90% of requests respond within 2 seconds.

Title: Validate response time for complex operations
Preconditions: App connected to Firebase with active data
Test Data: 5 complex operations (e.g., large data sync, intensive calculations)
Test Steps:
1. Execute each of the 5 complex operations.
2. Record the response time for each operation.
Expected Result: All operations respond within 5 seconds.

Title: Validate interface intuitiveness with test users
Preconditions: App installed on a test device, 5 representative users available
Test Data: 3 main tasks (e.g., login, data entry, navigation)
Test Steps:
1. Provide each user with the 3 main tasks to complete.
2. Observe and record any errors, confusion, or hesitation.
3. Count the number of users completing all tasks without errors.
Expected Result: At least 75% of users complete all tasks without errors or confusion.

Title: Verify Firebase database connection speed
Preconditions: App connected to Firebase, network stable
Test Data: 10 database read/write operations
Test Steps:
1. Execute 10 database operations (e.g., read, write, update).
2. Record the time taken for each operation.
Expected Result: All operations complete within an acceptable timeframe (no explicit threshold, but should align with overall performance goals).

Title: Validate secure data handling in Firebase
Preconditions: App connected to Firebase with sample user data (including sensitive data)
Test Data: Sample user data (e.g., name, email, password)
Test Steps:
1. Insert sample user data into Firebase via the app.
2. Verify data encryption and access rules are enforced.
3. Attempt unauthorized access to the data.
Expected Result: Data is stored securely, and unauthorized access is blocked.

Title: Validate app behavior with unstable internet connection
Preconditions: App installed, device connected to a network with controlled instability
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app with a stable internet connection.
2. Simulate network disconnection (e.g., via airplane mode or network throttling).
3. Attempt to perform operations requiring internet (e.g., data sync, login).
4. Reconnect and verify data integrity.
Expected Result: App handles disconnection gracefully (e.g., offline mode, error messages) and resumes correctly upon reconnection.

Title: Verify Minimum Device Requirements for App Installation
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with OS version below 8.1, 1GB RAM, 300MB storage
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to install Alluce Verde on a device running Android 7.1 (API 25).
2. Attempt to install on a device with 750MB RAM.
3. Attempt to install on a device with 200MB free storage.
Expected Result:
1. Installation fails with an error message indicating OS version is too low.
2. Installation fails with an error message indicating insufficient RAM.
3. Installation fails with an error message indicating insufficient storage.

Title: Verify App Functionality Without Internet Connection
Preconditions: App installed and opened
Test Data: Device with active airplane mode or no internet access
Test Steps:
1. Enable airplane mode on the device.
2. Attempt to open the app and navigate to any feature requiring data (e.g., plant upload, profile access).
3. Disable airplane mode and retry the same steps.
Expected Result:
1. App opens but critical features (e.g., upload, sync) are disabled or show an error message.
2. Error message: "No internet connection. Some features are unavailable."
3. Features re-enable and function normally after reconnecting.

Title: Verify Rejection of Irrelevant Plant Photos
Preconditions: User logged in with upload permissions
Test Data: Photo of a non-plant object (e.g., animal, food)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the upload feature.
2. Attempt to upload a photo of a cat labeled as a "plant."
3. Submit the upload.
Expected Result:
1. Upload process initiates.
2. System rejects the upload with an error message: "Photo must be relevant to plants. Please upload a valid plant image."
3. Photo is not saved to the database.

Title: Verify Firebase Data Security and Privacy
Preconditions: Firebase database configured with test data
Test Data: Sample user data (name, email, plant photos) with and without personal identifiers
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to access Firebase database directly using unauthorized credentials.
2. Attempt to upload user data with exposed personal information (e.g., full address).
3. Verify data encryption in transit and at rest.
Expected Result:
1. Access denied with error: "Unauthorized. Contact support for assistance."
2. System rejects upload with error: "Personal data must be protected. Remove sensitive information."
3. Data is encrypted during transmission and storage (verified via Firebase security rules).

Title: Verify User Feedback Collection via Google Form
Preconditions: Google Form linked to Alluce Verde feedback feature
Test Data: Sample responses (e.g., "Add a care calendar," "Improve search for propagated plants")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the feedback section in the app.
2. Submit a suggestion: "Add a feature to track watering schedules."
3. Verify the response appears in the linked Google Form.
4. Check if responses are anonymized in the form.
Expected Result:
1. Feedback submission button is functional.
2. Confirmation message: "Thank you for your feedback! It has been submitted."
3. Response appears in Google Form under the correct question.
4. User’s name/email is not visible in the form responses.
